steps: Go to https://dev.azure.com and sign in with your Microsoft account
Click your profile avatar in the top-right corner
Select 'Personal access tokens' from the dropdown menu
Enter a name for the token and select your organization
Under Scopes, select the permissions your use case needs (e.g. Code: Read, Build: Read & execute)
Copy the token immediately — it will not be shown again
To use the token as a Basic auth header, base64-encode the string ':YOUR_TOKEN' (colon prefix + token) and use it as the Authorization header value

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Personal Access Token (PAT)
credential_fields.description: Your Azure DevOps PAT. Used with Basic auth: base64-encode ':PAT' and pass as 'Authorization: Basic <encoded>'

notes: Azure DevOps is free for up to 5 users with Basic access; additional users require paid plans
Token is shown only once at creation — copy and store it securely
The token uses HTTP Basic auth with an empty username: base64(':' + token)
PAT expiration maximum is 1 year; set calendar reminders to rotate
Prefer scoped tokens over full-access tokens to limit blast radius
You can also use the direct URL: https://dev.azure.com/_usersSettings/tokens

test_endpoint.description: List all projects in your Azure DevOps organization
